# AGENTS.md — Generated by Niyam v0.1.0
# Tool: cursor | Generated: 2026-07-02
# Modules: rust, backend, clean-code, performance, git, testing

---

## Stack: Rust
- Use clippy with `#![deny(clippy::all)]`. Fix all warnings before commit.
- Prefer returning Result<T, E> over panicking. Reserve panic for unrecoverable states.
- Use strong newtypes for domain concepts (UserId, Email) — not bare primitives.
- Minimize unsafe. Every unsafe block gets a // SAFETY: comment explaining the invariant.
- Prefer &str over String in function parameters when ownership isn't needed.

## Role: Backend
- Design APIs contract-first. Define types and traits before implementation.
- Use structured logging (tracing crate) with span contexts for traceability.
- Handle partial failures gracefully: timeouts, retries with backoff, circuit breakers.
- Keep main.rs thin — delegate to lib.rs and domain modules immediately.

## Principles: Clean Code
- Names reveal intent. No abbreviations unless domain-standard.
- Functions do one thing. If you need "and" to describe it, split it.
- No duplication. Extract, don't copy-paste.
- Explicit over implicit. No hidden side effects or magic behavior.
- Separate commands from queries. A function that answers should not mutate.

## Principles: Performance
- Measure before optimizing. No premature optimization without profiling data.
- Prefer stack allocation over heap. Use &[T] over Vec<T> in function signatures.
- Avoid unnecessary clones. Use Cow<str> when ownership is conditional.
- Profile with `cargo flamegraph`. Benchmark with `criterion`.
- Prefer iterators over manual loops — the compiler optimizes them better.

## Workflow: Git
- Commits: conventional format (feat:, fix:, chore:). Subject under 72 chars.
- Branch per feature. Rebase onto main before merge.
- Never commit target/, .env, or Cargo.lock for libraries (do commit for binaries).
- Run `cargo fmt` and `cargo clippy` before every commit.

## Workflow: Testing
- Unit tests in the same file (`#[cfg(test)]` module). Integration tests in tests/.
- Use proptest or quickcheck for property-based testing on parsers and algorithms.
- Tests must be deterministic — no reliance on timing, network, or order.
- Name tests after the behavior: `test_rejects_empty_input`, not `test_parse`.
